# SSOT Registry

- The registry is the canonical source, canon, and authority record for governed software assurance entities.
- Boundaries freeze scoped delivery targets before certification and release.
- Claims link behavior to tests and evidence instead of relying on prose assertions.

## Pages
- [Ship from a registry that proves the release.](https://ssot-registry.com/) - A governed release-readiness registry for teams that need decisions, scope, proof, and publication state in one canonical record.
- [Start with the job, not the taxonomy](https://ssot-registry.com/content/) - Choose a practical SSOT Registry path: plan scope, prove release readiness, install or automate commands, or answer authority questions from the canonical registry.

- [What are ADRs in SSOT Registry?](https://ssot-registry.com/features/developer/adrs/what-is/) - ADRs matter because SSOT Registry turns them into named, normalized, inspectable SSOT records instead of leaving developers to reconstruct intent from scattered documents. This features guide explains the concept, shows where it fits in the SSOT canon, and points to practical next steps for a governed single source of truth. Explain ADRs and identify the next command-backed SSOT Registry step.
